title: Training with Kyle 5/5/19 Stretched all day

enrichment.summary: Kyle recounts spending an entire day doing his stretching routine for the first time in about 10 years, cycling through hamstring, split, calf, and upper body stretches from 11am to 8pm while listening to an audiobook. He mentions dealing with asthma and insomnia the night before, and plans to swim and bike ride the next day.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: estimates about 500 pages per day is the rule of thumb when combining training with audiobook listening
values it highly but had neglected it; happy to have done a full day of it

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today was his very first day of stretching.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He was originally going to do some sport exercising but decided to stretch all day because he was really wasted yesterday and his legs were barely moving.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He got up at 10:30 because he had insomnia from asthma last night.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He had to get on health insurance again to get some asthma medication.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He listened to 'Popular History of Ireland' until the end of book nine.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to do books 10, 11, and 12 tomorrow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The general rule of thumb for training all day long and listening to audiobooks is about 500 pages a day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If somebody has the computer read the book to you, it's faster and you can do more.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He likes to do a book when he stretches or reads it from his phone.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He can do a sport like running or swimming while having one of his Kindles read to him.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He did some simple stretches including hamstring left, hamstring right, double hamstring, splits, calves individually, and two different kinds of buttock ones.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has a demo video from 10 years ago with the same exact routine now, which involves isolating muscles and squeezing all the juices out of them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has two stretches for his upper body where he just hangs on something.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He cycled through his stretching routine from 11 until 8.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His legs are super sore, similar to lifting weights all day long.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It was like 10 years since the last time he went stretching.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wanted to see how limber he could get in one day and get his legs to a reasonable limberity.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His legs were a wreck the last couple of days.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wanted to get into the habit of stretching all the time but couldn't in the last couple of days because he was too tired.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is happy that he stretched and feels tired like he's been exercising all day long.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's midnight right now and he's been up for about 13 and a half hours.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to do some exercise tomorrow, possibly starting with swimming and then going on a ride to see how his bike computer's Strava segments work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He would like to film his exercise tomorrow.
